Phase 3 - Direct, Indirect & Headlines
FB Ad Copywriting
In this final segment to our three part training we explore the difference between
direct and indirect copywriting.
Direct copywriting: doesn't try to "break the ice" and instead talks straight to the
problem/solution and product.
Indirect copywriting: focuses more on the transformational story
When pulling headlines together, refer back to your worksheet and - depending on
what part of the funnel sequence you are writing for - base your copy on the
distinctions made in that level of awareness.
Generally speaking teasers and cliff hangers work well but make sure you aren't being
click bait with your headlines as Facebook could penalise you for this.
